title: End of Evangelion and Funimation License Podcast Interview
display_title: End of Evangelion and Funimation License Podcast Interview
wikitext:
[]: End of Evangelion and Funimation License? Podcast Interview!             Quote Post by cody727kirby » Thu Oct 28, 2010 11:40 am According to this ANNCast interview with Lance Heiskell, the Funimation marketing director at Funimation, Funimation have recently tried to obtain the license for the End of Evangelion film, but has been having a tough time getting all the features along with it. End of Evangelion licensing question (asked by V - UGH!) starts at 84:55 http://www.animenewsnetwork.com/anncast/2010-10-22 Just a sidenote, GREAT thing to hear was that Evangelion 1.11 was Funimation's BEST SELLER! (63:40) . PLUS Evangelion 2.22 DUBBING/CASTING information will be revealed in spring! (84:20)

[]:             Quote Post by The Eva Monkey » Thu Oct 28, 2010 11:53 am I enjoy the way Zac refers to V. He's clearly not a fan either.

[]:             Quote Post by Jayfive » Thu Oct 28, 2010 5:15 pm Question from someone completely new to the anime community, let alone the EVA community: Is this V as in the RevofE guy? The guy with all the youtube vids of convention presentations analyzing the show and stuff? Why dont people (and by the tone of the posts im thinking its most people) dont like him? Edit: Ah...word filter on the name of the guy's website. Im thinking theres some history here. Short version will do :P

[]:             Quote Post by KnightmareX13 » Thu Oct 28, 2010 5:19 pm Jayfive wrote: Is this V as in the snausages guy? The guy with all the youtube vids of convention presentations analyzing the show and stuff? Why dont people (and by the tone of the posts im thinking its most people) dont like him?

[]: *crosses fingers* Tiffanygranttiffanygranttiffanygrant--             Quote Post by Killer Bee » Thu Oct 28, 2010 5:25 pm cody727kirby wrote: GREAT thing to hear was that Evangelion 1.11 was Funimation's BEST SELLER!

[]:             Quote Post by Jayfive » Thu Oct 28, 2010 5:29 pm Double negatives are awesome.

[]:             Quote Post by IrkenEvangelion » Thu Oct 28, 2010 5:30 pm Hopefully they'll get the rights soon and release a BD. I want to start a collection of anime BD. I still don't have 1.11 yet. I bought the 1.01 on the day it came out and really haven't been motivated to get 1.11. Unfortunately I missed a lot of the banning around here (like V and Zuggy(I didn't even know V made his own site until Eva Monkey mentioned "Rudolph")), that was during my 'break' from EvaGeeks for about a year there. Problem was that there were going to be too many new posts to check when I came back so I kept postponing it.

[]: No one badgers my Tiffany Grant. NO ONE. >:[             Quote Post by Killer Bee » Thu Oct 28, 2010 5:35 pm Jayfive wrote: Also see the vid of him badgering Tiffany Grant about stuff like narcissistic personality disorder when shes trying to have her freakin lunch.

[]:             Quote Post by Hyper Shinchan » Thu Oct 28, 2010 5:54 pm Could I know what is said about V in that podcast or at least the point in which he is mentioned? I missed the entire drama about V and his ban but I've been more or less filled about the details by other members... Double negatives are awesome.

[]:             Quote Post by Jayfive » Thu Oct 28, 2010 6:12 pm Could I know what is said about V in that podcast or at least the point in which he is mentioned?

[]:             Quote Post by Hyper Shinchan » Thu Oct 28, 2010 6:33 pm Jayfive wrote: 82:48 - 'we've got a couple here from everybody's favourite.....Evangelion V guy...you're familiar with his antics im sure....' Miaow.

[]:             Quote Post by IrkenEvangelion » Thu Oct 28, 2010 6:37 pm It's not that they hate him. They just don't know what kind of a guy he is. He acted like an idiot on here and rubbed everyone the wrong way giving a VERY bad impression on everyone. So anyone in the Eva fan community sees him as not to smart of a cookie while everyone "outside of the circle" doesn't see what the big deal is... Anyways.... what are some of the special features they're having issues getting?

[]:             Quote Post by The Eva Monkey » Thu Oct 28, 2010 7:17 pm Hyper Shinchan wrote: Thank you. Anyway he still manages to get his question answered by these guys in the industry, maybe they don't hate him that much.

[]:             Quote Post by tomrule123 » Thu Oct 28, 2010 7:44 pm THAT... WOULD MAKE... MY DAY... IF THIS WERE TO HAPPEN. DON'T GIVE UP, FUN IMATION ! ...Plus, I hope the Renewal of EoE was remastered in raw HD. If that weren't the case and Funi then creates an Upscaled Blu-ray, that would ruin my expectations on my next revisit to the film.

[]:             Quote Post by Hyper Shinchan » Thu Oct 28, 2010 7:50 pm IrkenEvangelion wrote: It's not that they hate him. They just don't know what kind of a guy he is. He acted like an idiot on here and rubbed everyone the wrong way giving a VERY bad impression on everyone. So anyone in the Eva fan community sees him as not to smart of a cookie while everyone "outside of the circle" doesn't see what the big deal is...

[]:             Quote Post by The Eva Monkey » Thu Oct 28, 2010 8:05 pm Hyper Shinchan wrote: Well, he has his own userbase on his website

[]:             Quote Post by tomrule123 » Thu Oct 28, 2010 8:19 pm The Eva Monkey wrote: REMASTERD ENDS OF EVANJELLIONS DO WANT

[]:             Quote Post by child of Lilith » Thu Oct 28, 2010 8:23 pm I can’t wait for this to come out. It’ll be great to own a copy of EOE that doesn’t look like crap.

[]:             Quote Post by The Eva Monkey » Thu Oct 28, 2010 8:44 pm I hope this isn't a repeat of when Manga Entertainment tried to do the same: http://www.animenewsnetwork.com/news/2006-07-22/comic-con-manga-reveals-appp-project-movie-plans

[]:             Quote Post by Jayfive » Fri Oct 29, 2010 8:52 am Heh, i tell you what he who should not be named put me in mind of: A few years ago there was a one-off comedy-drama on UK tv called 'Cruise of the Gods' - it was about 2 actors (one big in hollywood, the other washed up) who appeared as teenagers 20-30 years earlier in a obscure childrens sci-fi show that got canned after 1 series. They are invited onto a fan-cruise and surrounded by fans utterly obsessed with the show. Clip: http://www.youtube.com/watch?v=6mrsUSfrxGo Towards the end theres a presentation by the organiser of the cruise and the biggest uber-fan going through in great and insistent detail about the significance of the names of the characters. Whereupon the writer of the show (a drunken old hack invited on the cruise) explains that all the names are simply anagrams of types of curry and the only significance is that he was done the local indian restaurant while he was finishing off writing the show... Apparently this is being remade for the US as per July this year as 'The Great Beyond' but I urge you to seek out the original by fair means or foul :)
